communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I hereby request copies of all records in the custody or control of the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) — and any predecessor agency, including the Office of Strategic Services (OSS) — that relate to, mention, reference, or were generated in connection with the individual described below.

Subject of Request -

Full Name: David Peter Abashidze
Also known as / name variants: Davit Abashidze; D.P. Abashidze; Prince Abashidze; Давид Петрович Абашидзе (Cyrillic)
Title: Prince (Knyaz), of the Georgian noble House of Abashidze
Date of Birth: July 17, 1878
Place of Birth: Tiflis (Tbilisi), Georgia, Imperial Russian Empire
Date of Death: July 11, 1952
Place of Death: New York City, New York, United States
Nationality at Death: Naturalized United States citizen
Business Affiliation: Caucasian-American Mining & Trading Corporation (New York)
Proof of death is enclosed: a copy of the obituary published in The New York Times on July 12, 1952, confirming the subject's death and the above biographical details.
Link to obit: https://www.nytimes.com/1952/07/12/archives/david-p-abasifidzu-a-georgian-prince.html

Records Sought - 

I request all responsive records regardless of format (paper, microfilm, electronic, index cards, or any other medium), including but not limited to:

Any name-indexed files, subject files, or personality files concerning David Peter Abashidze or the Caucasian-American Mining & Trading Corporation;
Any records generated or received during the period 1940–1952 relating to Mr. Abashidze's business dealings with Soviet state entities or Soviet-affiliated trading partners, including cables, memoranda, reports, assessments, or contact reports;
Any records generated or received during the OSS period (1941–1945) referencing Mr. Abashidze, whether in the context of émigré intelligence, Caucasian affairs, or Soviet commercial penetration;
Any records referencing Mr. Abashidze's multilingual capabilities (he was known to speak Georgian, Russian, and English) or his potential utility as a source, contact, or liaison;
Any records relating to the House of Abashidze or Georgian émigré networks in New York City in which David Peter Abashidze is named or can be reasonably identified;
Any index entries, card files, or database entries in the CIA's Central Reference and Records Facility (CRRF) or any predecessor index system referencing the above name variants or the Caucasian-American Mining & Trading Corporation;
Any liaison referrals or records shared with, received from, or jointly generated with the Federal Bureau of Investigation, the State Department, the Office of Naval Intelligence, or Military Intelligence (G-2) concerning the above subject.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I hereby request all records maintained by the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) pertaining to the following individual:

Full Name: David Peter Abashidze 
Title: Prince
Date of Birth: July 17th 1878 - Tbilisi, Georgia (Imperial Russian Empire) 
Date of Death: July 11 1952 - New York City 

Proof of death: David Peter Abashidze's death was widely reported and his obituary appeared in the July 12th 1953 edition of the New York Times. Abashidze was the owner of the "Caucasian-American Mining & Trading Corporation" at the time of this death. He was a rare, private U.S. citizen who had business contracts with the Soviet state. 

Link to obit: https://www.nytimes.com/1952/07/12/archives/david-p-abasifidzu-a-georgian-prince.html

I am requesting a search of the Central Records System (CRS) for both "main files" and "cross-reference" files. This request specifically includes, but is not limited to, correspondence, internal memoranda, surveillance logs, and investigative reports where Abashidze is the subject or is mentioned in relation to other individuals. Please conduct a search for Abashidze records from any and all FBI offices, including HQ, field offices, legal attaché offices worldwide, and facilities that maintain records under the FBI’s control.

Please conduct a search for any correspondence (either to or from) Abashidze and the following bureau officials:

J. Edgar Hoover
Clyde Tolson
Edward Tamm
D. Milton Ladd 

According to a blog post read, it is important that I state the following: "As you know, the Federal Bureau of Investigation is required to search the records stored at NARA’s Washington Records Center in Suitland, MD, but still technically under the FBI’s control. For more information see 36 CFR 1250.8" Please also conduct a physical search of records at NARA's Washington Records Center in Suitland, for any records or documents related to David Peter Abashidze. 

If the bureau cannot fulfill certain parts of this request or certain portions must be redacted please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Thank you to the bureau for the commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware
